channel.title: Technology - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: The 13 biggest announcements at Google I/O 2026 - The Verge
item.description:

item.description:

  2. The new iPhone Ultra and iPhone 18 lineup: Everything you need to know about Apple's rumored 2026 launches  Yahoo Tech
  3. Foldable iPhone Production Stalls Amid Hinge Issues  MacRumors
  4. iPhone Ultra will take us closer to the long-rumored iPad Fold, suggests leaker  9to5Mac
  5. 'Visually creaseless': the iPhone Ultra is tipped to have an industry-leading foldable screen — but hinge problems could see the phone delayed until 2027  TechRadar

item.title: The iPhone and Mac security Apple spent 5 years building? AI broke it in 5 days. - Cult of Mac
item.description:
1. The iPhone and Mac security Apple spent 5 years building? AI broke it in 5 days.  Cult of Mac
  2. Apple’s Security Has Been Tough to Crack. Mythos Helped Find a Way In.  WSJ
  3. Researchers Claim Anthropic's Mythos Helped Crack macOS Security  PCMag
  4. First Apple M5 memory exploit discovered using Anthropic AI, gives root access on MacOS — Claude Mythos helps security researchers bypass Memory Integrity Enforcement  Tom's Hardware
  5. Security Researchers, Aided By Anthropic's Mythos, Claim To Have Breached macOS  Engadget

item.title: The Dory Sign is E ink, smart screen simplicity at its finest - Ars Technica
item.description: The Dory Sign is E ink, smart screen simplicity at its finest  Ars Technica

  3. The Original Chromebook Dream Is Dead. Googlebooks Killed It  PCMag
  4. Googlebook Is Google’s New AI-Powered Laptop Platform Built on Android  WIRED
  5. Google just helped Apple sell a million more MacBook Neos  Macworld

item.title: After United Canceled Her Flight, An Agent Said “Nothing” Could Be Done — But She Heard The Trash Talk In The Background - View from the Wing

item.title: Torvalds: AI Tools Great When Not Causing Unnecessary Pain & Pointless Make-Believe Work - Phoronix
item.description:
1. Torvalds: AI Tools Great When Not Causing Unnecessary Pain & Pointless Make-Believe Work  Phoronix
  2. Linus Torvalds says AI-powered bug hunters have made Linux security mailing list ‘almost entirely unmanageable’  The Register
  3. Linus Torvalds says flood of duplicate AI-generated vulnerability reports have made Linux security mailing list 'almost entirely unmanageable' — private list 'a waste of time for everybody involved' in switch to new public system  Tom's Hardware
  4. Linux developers are getting bombarded with AI-generated bug reports, and Linus isn't happy  XDA
  5. AI is drowning software maintainers in junk security reports  Help Net Security
